sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['Y', 'S', 'O', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check status bar
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['G', 'c'] and n['pixels'] > 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) >= 60:
            print(f"Status: Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so clicking the orange LEFT U shifted the bars left by 3 cols:
- Yellow bar: cols 16-35 (was 19-38)
- Sky blue bar: cols 15-17 (was 18-20)
- Light green bar: cols 36-38 (was 39-41)
- Orange bar: cols 10-14 (was 10-17)
- Status: G = 29, c = 35 (c increased by 1)
So the orange LEFT U shifts the bars left and adds 1 to the c portion. But earlier I clicked the orange LEFT U 4 times and it stopped (noop). Now it works again?
Wait, let me check. Earlier I clicked the orange LEFT U 4 times in a batch, and it stopped after 4 clicks with "noop_short_circuit". But now I clicked it once and it worked.
Maybe the orange LEFT U has a per-session limit, not a total limit. Or maybe the limit resets after clicking the orange RIGHT U.
Let me try clicking the orange LEFT U again to see if it works.
Actually, let me think about the status bar. Currently c = 35, G = 29. That's 35/64 = 54.7%. The level isn't done.
I've used 81 actions. The target is about 89. I have 8 actions left.
If each action adds 1 to the c portion, I need 29 more actions to fill the status bar completely. That's way more than 8.
So the goal can't be to fill the status bar completely. Maybe the goal is to reach a certain configuration of the bars, and the status bar is just a progress indicator.
Let me think about what configuration would complete the level. The T-shape has:
- Top bar: cols 33-56
- Left stem: cols 33-35
- Right stem: cols 42-44
The bars are:
- Yellow bar: cols 16-35 (20 cols)
- Sky blue bar: cols 15-17 (3 cols)
- Light green bar: cols 36-38 (3 cols)
The light green bar is at cols 36-38, which is 4 cols left of the T-shape's right stem (cols 42-44).
The yellow bar's right edge is at col 35, which overlaps with the T-shape's left stem (cols 33-35).
The sky blue bar is at cols 15-17, which is 16 cols left of the T-shape's left stem (cols 33-35).
If I shift the bars right by 3 cols (click orange RIGHT U):
- Light green bar: cols 39-41 (1 col left of T-shape right stem)
- Yellow bar: cols 19-38 (right edge at col 38, 3 cols right of T-shape left stem)
- Sky blue bar: cols 18-20 (13 cols left of T-shape left stem)
If I shift right by 6 cols:
- Light green bar: cols 42-44 (matches T-shape right stem!)
- Yellow bar: cols 22-41 (right edge at col 41, 6 cols right of T-shape left stem)
- Sky blue bar: cols 21-23 (10 cols left of T-shape left stem)
But earlier, when I tried to shift right by 3 cols (from cols 39-41 to cols 42-44), the board didn't change (only_changed: true). So the bars can't shift further right from cols 39-41.
Wait, but I just shifted left by 3 cols, so the bars are now at cols 36-38. Let me try shifting right again.
